title: PostToolUse output suppression: two-channel display for all 7 MCP tools
description: Raw MCP JSON currently leaks into conversation for all 7 beadle-email tools (list_messages, read_message, send_email, list_folders, show_mime, verify_signature, check_trust). Implement PostToolUse suppress-output.sh with two-channel display: compact summary in updatedMCPToolOutput, full data in additionalContext. Classify each tool as silent (confirmations like send_email) or data (lists like list_messages). Follow punt-kit two-channel-display pattern.
close_reason: suppress-output.sh implemented with two-channel display for all 7 MCP tools. Silent: send_email (panel-only). Data: list_messages, read_message, list_folders, show_mime, verify_signature, check_trust (summary + additionalContext). Handles null results, errors, and both plugin/standalone tool name prefixes.

title: MCP tool output leaks raw JSON in standalone mode
description: When beadle-email is registered via 'claude mcp add' (standalone, not plugin), MCP tool responses show raw JSON in the conversation instead of formatted two-channel display. The PostToolUse suppress-output hook only fires for plugin installations where hooks.json is loaded. Standalone MCP registrations have no hook mechanism. Need to investigate how biff handles this — likely the MCP server itself needs to format output, not rely on hooks. See punt-kit standards for two-channel display pattern.
close_reason: Merged in PR #40. MCP tools return pre-formatted text instead of raw JSON.

title: Identity entity: multi-identity support for beadle
description: Beadle reads identity from ethos sidecar (DES-001). Core fields (email, name, handle) from ~/.punt-labs/ethos/identities/<handle>.yaml. Beadle-specific data (gpg_key_id, contact permissions) stored in ethos extension mechanism (DES-008) at <handle>.ext/beadle.yaml — beadle owns this file, ethos assembles merged view but never interprets it. Access via 'ethos ext set/get <handle> beadle <key> <value>'. Multi-identity = switching ethos active identity. Beadle maps email to IMAP config internally. No identity management inside beadle. No ethos schema changes.
close_reason: Closed

title: Integration test layer: MCP server + in-process IMAP/SMTP
description: Replace the 'live (manual)' test layer with automated integration tests. Three levels: (1) MCP smoke — start binary, JSON-RPC over stdio, verify tool registration and response schemas. (2) IMAP/SMTP integration — in-process test mail server (go-imap/go-smtp), test send→list→read→verify flow with ephemeral GPG keys. (3) End-to-end — MCP subprocess talking to test mail server. Keep optional Proton Bridge test behind PROTON_BRIDGE_AVAILABLE env var. Target: bring mcp package from 14.8% to 60%+ and email from 34% to 60%+ via integration coverage.

title: make dist must generate checksums.txt
description: make dist cross-compiles 4 binaries but does not generate checksums.txt. install.sh requires checksums.txt for SHA256 verification (step 4). v0.5.0 and v0.6.0 were both released without checksums — v0.6.0 was manually patched by uploading checksums after the fact. Fix: add shasum -a 256 to make dist. Also add checksums.txt generation to the release workflow in CLAUDE.md so it's not missed again.
close_reason: PR #58 merged: make dist generates checksums.txt, backfilled v0.5.0 and v0.6.0

title: Docs and descriptions out of sync
description:
PR #1 review feedback. Four doc issues:

1. `send_email` tool description says "via Resend API" — should say "via Proton Bridge SMTP with Resend fallback"
2. README features list mentions "search" tool that doesn't exist — remove
3. `email-channel-plan.md` config snippet has `imap_password_file` and `resend_api_key_file` fields that don't exist in implementation
4. `verify.go` `Verify()` uses `os.MkdirTemp("")` which can exceed gpg-agent socket path length on macOS — use `/tmp` like tests do

Files: `internal/mcp/tools.go`, `README.md`, `docs/email-channel-plan.md`, `internal/pgp/verify.go`
close_reason: Closed

title: Add cc and bcc support to send_email
description: The send_email MCP tool only accepts a single 'to' field. Add cc and bcc parameters (both optional string or []string). Requires changes to: (1) MCP tool definition in internal/mcp/tools.go, (2) SMTP sender in internal/email/ to set Cc/Bcc headers, (3) Resend sender fallback if applicable. Requested by @jmf-pobox via biff — needed for multi-recipient threads (e.g. balance reports to jim@punt-labs.com and atriafreeman@proton.me).
close_reason: cc/bcc support implemented in PR #27

title: PGP-signed outbound email via Amazon SES
description:
## Problem

Beadle cannot send PGP-signed emails that recipients' mail clients (GPG Mail, Thunderbird, etc.) auto-verify. Two transports were tested:

1. **Proton Bridge SMTP** — strips `multipart/signed` envelope during re-processing. Signature arrives as a detached attachment, not RFC 3156 PGP/MIME. GPG Mail does not auto-verify.
2. **Resend API** — no raw MIME support. Only accepts structured fields (to, from, subject, text/html). Cannot send pre-composed `multipart/signed` messages.

Inbound PGP verification works correctly (tested with real GPG Mail signed email, UID 5).

## Solution

Add Amazon SES as the primary outbound transport for external recipients. SES `SendRawEmail` accepts raw RFC 822 messages and delivers them without modifying MIME structure.

### Sender chain (new)

1. **SES + PGP sign** — external recipients. `pgp.Sign()` composes `multipart/signed`, SES delivers intact. GPG Mail auto-verifies.
2. **Proton Bridge SMTP** — Proton-to-Proton recipients. E2E encryption (better than PGP). No PGP signing needed.
3. **Resend plain** — last resort fallback if both SES and Bridge are down.

### DNS changes (punt-labs.com)

- **SPF**: `v=spf1 include:_spf.protonmail.ch include:amazonses.com ~all`
- **DKIM**: 3 CNAME records from SES domain verification
- **DMARC**: no change needed (`p=quarantine` already set)

### Code changes

1. **`internal/email/ses.go`** — `SESSend(cfg, from, to, rawMIME)` using AWS SDK v2 `sesv2.SendEmail` with `RawMessage`
2. **`internal/email/config.go`** — add `SESRegion` field (default `us-east-1`)
3. **`internal/secret/`** — add `ses-access-key-id` and `ses-secret-access-key` credential names
4. **`internal/mcp/tools.go`** — update `trySendChain()`: SES+GPG → Bridge → Resend
5. **`cmd/beadle-email/main.go`** — add SES checks to `doctor` and `status`
6. **`pgp/sign.go`** — already built and tested, no changes needed

### Credential storage

- `ses-access-key-id` → macOS Keychain via `security add-generic-password -s beadle -a ses-access-key-id -w '...'`
- `ses-secret-access-key` → same pattern
- `gpg-passphrase` → already stored

### Dependencies

- AWS SDK v2: `github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2` + `config` + `credentials` + `service/sesv2`
- punt-labs.com domain must be verified in SES
- SES must be out of sandbox (or recipient must be verified)

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `send_email` to external address arrives with `multipart/signed` envelope intact
- [ ] GPG Mail (Apple Mail) shows green verification banner on received message
- [ ] `beadle-email doctor` shows SES status
- [ ] Proton-to-Proton messages still go through Bridge (no SES detour)
- [ ] All existing tests pass, new SES integration test added
- [ ] SPF/DKIM/DMARC all pass for SES-sent messages (check via message headers)

### Manual setup steps (before code)

1. Verify `punt-labs.com` in SES console → gives 3 DKIM CNAMEs
2. Add SES DKIM CNAMEs to DNS
3. Update SPF record: `v=spf1 include:_spf.protonmail.ch include:amazonses.com ~all`
4. Wait for DNS propagation (~5-10 min for most registrars)
5. Create IAM user with `ses:SendRawEmail` permission only
6. Store credentials: `security add-generic-password -s beadle -a ses-access-key-id -w '...'` and same for `ses-secret-access-key`
7. Check SES sandbox status — sandbox limits sending to verified addresses only. Request production access if needed.
8. Verify a test recipient address if still in sandbox

title: Consolidate filesystem paths to ~/.punt-labs/beadle/

title: Command naming design: resolve namespace conflicts with biff/vox, define beadle-email commands
description:
Three command verbs for beadle-email:

/mail — 'Mail this to me/someone.' Email-specific outbound. Always means email. Examples: '/mail me a summary', '/mail this to kai@example.com'.

/inbox — 'Process your inbox.' Beadle checks its own inbox for owner instructions/context. Examples: '/inbox', '/inbox check for anything from me'.

/send — 'Send via any channel.' Multi-channel outbound, email-only today but designed for Signal etc. Examples: '/send me an email', '/send me a link on Signal'.

Today /mail and /send overlap (both send email). They diverge when new channels arrive: /send routes by channel, /mail always means email.

/read stays with biff today. Future: becomes multi-channel inbox (biff + email + Signal).

Namespace: beadle:mail, beadle:inbox, beadle:send as plugin-scoped. /mail, /inbox, /send as top-level deployed commands. No conflicts with existing biff/vox/quarry/z-spec commands.

MCP server key: 'email' (adds meaning like biff uses 'tty', dungeon uses 'grimoire'). Tool name simplification (send_email → send, etc.) deferred.
close_reason: Command naming resolved: /mail (email outbound), /inbox (process inbox), /send (multi-channel). Documented in DESIGN.md DES-010.

title: fix(docs): update README install SHA to v0.6.1
close_reason: PR with README SHA fix

title: Security hardening: timeouts, TLS, path traversal
description:
PR #1 review feedback. Five related hardening issues:

1. `secret.Get()` should reject path separators in name and enforce file permissions before reading
2. IMAP `Dial()` uses `net.Dial` with no timeout — use `net.DialTimeout`
3. IMAP/SMTP `InsecureSkipVerify` should only be allowed for loopback addresses
4. `Send()` uses `http.DefaultClient` with no timeout — create client with 30s timeout
5. SMTP `net.Dial` needs timeout, magic number `2*1e9` should be `2*time.Second`

Files: `internal/secret/secret.go`, `internal/email/imap.go`, `internal/email/smtp.go`, `internal/email/send.go`
close_reason: Closed

title: list_messages unread_only filter returns null for unread messages
close_reason: Fixed in PR #22 — UIDSearch + empty slice return

title: CLI parity: add list, read, send, move subcommands
close_reason: Merged in PR #38. CLI parity (list/read/send/move/folders), global flags (--json/--verbose/--quiet), install/uninstall subcommands.

title: Code quality: typed responses, UTF-8 truncation, boundary parsing
description:
PR #1 review feedback. Three code quality issues:

1. `tools.go` uses `map[string]any` for tool responses — define typed structs per CLAUDE.md Go standards
2. `truncate()` in `mime.go` uses byte slicing — splits multi-byte UTF-8 runes. Use rune-aware slicing.
3. `extractSignedParts` in `verify.go` uses `bytes.Split` on boundary — can mis-split if boundary appears in body. Should anchor on CRLF + boundary at line start.

Files: `internal/mcp/tools.go`, `internal/email/mime.go`, `internal/pgp/verify.go`
close_reason: Closed

title: Plugin scaffold: .claude-plugin/plugin.json, hooks/, commands/

Create the scaffold: .claude-plugin/plugin.json (name, version, mcpServers for beadle-email), hooks/ directory with hooks.json, commands/ directory. Follow punt-kit plugin standards. The mcpServers entry should reference the beadle-email binary with stdio transport. Dev name: beadle-dev, prod name: beadle.

Potential commands: check inbox, send email, send conversation summary. Each command needs proper allowed-tools declaration referencing mcp__plugin_beadle_email__ tools.

title: SessionStart hook: auto-allow MCP permissions, deploy commands, first-run setup

Must: (1) auto-allow beadle-email MCP tool permissions in ~/.claude/settings.json, (2) deploy top-level commands from commands/ to ~/.claude/commands/ using diff-and-copy pattern, (3) emit hookSpecificOutput with additionalContext describing setup state. Shell script is thin gate, delegates to beadle-email binary. Must handle both dev and prod plugin namespaces.

description: beadle-email has no CLI or MCP tool to show or set the per-repo identity. Users must manually create .punt-labs/ethos/config.yaml. This causes permission errors when the repo's ethos active identity differs from the one with contacts/permissions configured. Add: beadle-email identity (show resolved identity + source), beadle-email identity set <handle> (write per-repo ethos config). May also need a corresponding MCP tool so Claude Code sessions can self-diagnose identity issues.

SessionStart hook reads inbox_poll key (default 30m, valid: 5m/10m/15m/30m/1h/2h/n) and emits CronCreate instruction. /inbox command gains interval setting (/inbox 5m), disable (/inbox n), and status (/inbox status). Config persists in project-root .claude/beadle.local.md (gitignored). PR #30.

title: archive_message MCP tool: move messages to Archive folder
description: Beadle-email has no way to manage inbox after reading messages. Add an archive_message tool that moves a message from the current folder to the Archive folder via IMAP MOVE (or COPY+DELETE for servers that don't support MOVE). This is needed for /inbox workflow — after processing messages, beadle should archive them so they don't reappear. Must update suppress-output.sh with a handler for the new tool (silent: 'archived message #N').
close_reason: Implemented as move_message (general-purpose, destination defaults to Archive). IMAP client uses go-imap/v2 Move with built-in COPY+STORE+EXPUNGE fallback. MCP tool, handler, and suppress-output handler all added. make check passes.

title: refactor(identity): remove legacy email.json fallback from resolution chain
description: The identity resolver has a step 6 fallback that reads from_address from ~/.punt-labs/beadle/email.json (source: 'legacy'). This predates ethos and the default-identity file. With ethos deployed and default-identity as a non-ethos fallback, the legacy path is dead code. Remove fromLegacy(), the legacyConfig struct, and the legacy IdentitySource value. Requires updating the Z spec (beadle-identity.tex) to remove the legacy variant from IdentitySource and the hasLegacy/legacyIdentity fields from IdentityStore.
close_reason: Closed

title: Address book: resolve recipients by name instead of raw email address
description: Every send_email call requires a raw email address. An address book would let commands use names: '/mail jim' resolves to jim@punt-labs.com, '/send kai the summary' resolves to kai@example.com. Storage: ~/.config/beadle/contacts.json or similar. Needs an MCP tool to query contacts (lookup by name) and a CLI command to manage them (add, remove, list). Enables natural language recipient resolution in /mail and /send commands.
close_reason: Merged in PR #31. Address book with name-based recipient resolution, 4 MCP tools, CLI parity, single-roundtrip send_email resolution.

title: Config tests fail on CI without credentials
description:
PR #1 review feedback.

`TestIMAPPassword_Resolves` and `TestResendAPIKey_Resolves` call real `secret.Get()` chain and assert no error. Fails on any machine without credentials configured. Use `t.Setenv` with test values instead.

Files: `internal/email/config_test.go`
close_reason: Closed

title: download_attachment: extract and return attachment content by part index
description: read_message lists attachment metadata (filename, content type, size) but cannot extract the actual content. Add a download_attachment tool that takes message_id and part index, extracts the MIME part bytes, and returns them (base64 for binary, text for text parts). Add suppress-output handler. Enables reading attached documents, saving files locally, forwarding attachments.
close_reason: Implemented in feat/download-attachment branch

title: Fix ethos repo config contract: active → agent
close_reason: Closed

title: PGP verification consistency: read_message and trust model fixes
description:
PR #1 review feedback. Three related issues:

1. `read_message` handler doesn't run PGP verification like `list_messages` — user sees `verified` in listing but `unverified` when reading the same message
2. `sign.go` declares `Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable` but writes body verbatim — change to `7bit`
3. Dead re-parse in `verify.go` L159-163 — `mail.ReadMessage` result immediately discarded

Files: `internal/mcp/tools.go`, `internal/pgp/sign.go`, `internal/pgp/verify.go`
close_reason: Closed

title: Add mid-session identity switching via MCP tool
Contact permissions: per-identity rwx trust model
description: Add permissions map to Contact struct: map[identity_email] → 'rwx'|'rw-'|'r--'|'---'. When processing inbound mail, gate behavior based on (current identity, sender) permissions combined with transport trust. Default r-- for unknown contacts. Owner always rwx. No inheritance between identities. See DES-012.
close_reason: Closed

title: send_email: support file attachments via multipart/mixed MIME
description: send_email only sends text/plain today. Add an attachments parameter (list of file paths) and build multipart/mixed MIME in ComposeRaw. Must work through both Proton Bridge SMTP and Resend API paths. Enables /mail me a PDF, /send me the test results as a file, etc.
close_reason: Merged in PR #15
